### Post by the dammed one on 2010-05-09
let me go here i have a book called ubuntu unleashed i have read from this book as needed. I feel that i still have a fundamental misunderstanding of linux systems. Where is a information source that will explain what is going on under the hood.

i got it to boot still no write permissions.
fstab is
# /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config -- 
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>

/dev/sdc5 /home ext3 rw,noexec,nosuid,nodev,relatime 0 0
/dev/sda1 /home/user8/01-my-data ext3 rw,noexec,nosuid,nodev,relatime 0 0

/dev/sde1 /media/sde1 vfat rw,noexec,nosuid,nodev 0 0

### Post by gzarkadas on 2010-05-11
Try to change your fstab as follows and see if it works; You will have to make user8 the owner of /dev/sda1 /dev/sdd2 with the `chown' command. 
**Note-1:** Though the documentation describes it, I have never used the `owner' staff; thus keep a note of previous owner of the devices to restore them if the need arises
**Note-2:** if the vfat drives in /media give you no issues, then ignore these changes.

If it does not work, please explain where you do not get write permissions. Also typing `man mount' provides a lot of relevant information.

```

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
# Entry for /dev/sdb1 :
UUID=13b75e9d-c41c-41ec-8b1b-6a9b5b0e79bb / ext3 relatime,rw,errors=remount-ro 0 1
# Entry for /dev/sdc5 :
UUID=6f66552b-6cbc-436d-ba8e-16827dcca47c /home ext3 **defaults,relatime,rw,errors=remount-ro** 0 2
# Entry for /dev/sda1 :
UUID=b7035b65-1e3f-45c8-a531-d19d7dd662f4 /home/user8/01-my-data ext3 **owner****,relatime,rw****,errors=remount-ro** 0 2
# Entry for /dev/sdd2 :
UUID=fb0ffa6c-040b-4e28-88c4-dcdf48a1857f /home/user8/02-my-data ext3 **owner,relatime,rw****,errors=remount-ro** 0 2
# Entry for /dev/sdc1 :
UUID=84309199-c331-40ea-b869-24a8d03fca56 none swap sw 0 0
/dev/scd0 /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto,exec,utf8 0 0
/dev/fd0 /media/floppy0 auto rw,user,noauto,exec,utf8 0 0
UUID=D2B6-7FFE /media/sdf1 vfat **umask=027,**user,rw 0 0
UUID=A6E4-887B /media/sde1 vfat **umask=027,**user,rw 0 0

```

---
